title: Jonathan Kugel
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Jonathan Kugel
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Professor Kugel is a Certified Public Accountant and previously practiced in the Federal government, Not-for-Profit, and Municipal government sectors. In addition to teaching a range of courses in the Accounting major, he conducts behavioral and experimental research in financial statement user responses to disclosures and how managers blend financial and non-financial incentives in monitoring employees. A proud former resident of Exit 8A, Professor Kugel enjoys fairly mild accounting humor, finding new recipes that honestly he'll never get right, and exploring the many fascinating historical and cultural sites around Tidewater.
education_entries: Ph D in Accounting, Georgia Institute of Technology
BS in Accounting, University of Maryland, College Park
research_interests: Experimental studies of (in)formal management control systems. Behavioral studies regarding not-for-profit & government financial statement user judgments and decisions.
teaching_interests: Auditing, Intermediate Accounting II, Financial Regulation & Professional Responsibility, Gov./NFP Accounting, and Writing Intensive courses.
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2026). Call Me Maybe? An Experimental Analysis of Donor Backlash to Voluntary Nonprofit Fraud Disclosure . Nonprofit Voluntary Sector Quarterly / Sage Publishing. Volume, Forthcoming.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2024). Good Governance in Not-for-Profit Organizations: A Review of the Literature on Boards of Directors . Journal of Governmental and Nonprofit Accounting. Volume, 13. Issue, 1. Pages, 56-82.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2023). The ChatGPT Artificial Intelligence Chatbot: How Well Does It Answer Accounting Assessment Questions? . Issues in Accounting Education. Volume, 38. Issue, 4. Pages, 81-108.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2023). A Synthesis of Accounting Studies that Examine Personality Variables . Emerald Publishing Limited. Volume, 26. Issue, 1. Pages, 325-348.
